summary: Solid 1994 show highlighted by a monster Eyes‑Estimated‑Terrapin stretch; soundboard capture is crisp and enjoyable.
review: Fans rave about the inspired second‑set jamming—especially the 50‑minute Eyes > Estimated > Terrapin suite—and praise Jerry’s engaged playing, Vince’s tasty keys and Bobby’s memorable solo. The recording is consistently described as nice or crisp, indicating a clean soundboard source with no major flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the performance highly (many 4‑5 stars) and note a clear, well‑balanced recording; a few low‑star outliers temper the average but do not outweigh the overall positive consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call it "nice" or "crisp" with no complaints about drop‑outs, hiss, or audience bleed – a clean mix that lets the band’s nuances shine.

standout_songs: Eyes of the World > Estimated > Terrapin
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid and cohesive, while the second set delivers a marathon jam (Eyes‑Estimated‑Terrapin) that many fans cite as the highlight; overall pacing is energetic and keeps listeners engaged.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Consistently described as highly engaged with memorable solos and a unique intro to Eyes of the World; even when his tone isn’t everyone’s favorite, his playing shines.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) steps in for a standout solo after Jerry breaks a string on SOTM and also delivers strong vocal work later in the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards are called "tasty" on Miracle, adding flavor to the jams.
